steps.title: Identify the transfer problem
steps.content: The passage says MFCs lose efficiency because electrons must pass through a series of inefficient redox reactions before reaching an electrode. The silver nanoparticles increased power density, and silver is highly conductive.

steps.title: Link conductivity to bypassing
steps.content: A focuses on bacterial metabolism, B makes the result independent of silver, and C suggests catalyzing the redox reactions rather than avoiding them. The logical hypothesis is that conductive silver lets electrons transfer directly to the electrode. The answer is D.
